So, psychosocibernetics happens to be a thing I found that I might just as easily have found something else. Now, having said that, the efficacy of psychosocibernetics is, first of all, it's conceptually correct, right? But beyond that, most personal development, self-improvement in this genre, most of it is all conceptual. Not in any way, it's all things to think, not things to do, right? And so, like, I needed something to do. And my first experience with it was getting control of a really severe stuttering problem. And for which traditional remedies had already failed. So, I've been to the speech therapist, I've been in the class, I use the machine, I got the metronome, all that shit. So, I needed something to do. I didn't need a conceptual philosophical discussion about, I mean, I needed, here's the six steps to use visualization in order to, right? So, that really is sort of unique to psychosocibernetics. So, had I found something else, I don't know. Yes, no, whatever. But it opens with, essentially, with random chance for most people. And then a big difference between most and a few is, do they go any further? Do they now dig historically deeper? Well, where did that come from? Maybe there's something that preceded it that could be useful to me. An upstream movement, well, who, right, a forward movement, most people don't do any of that, right? And so, like somebody who would see Tony, so let's take Tony, because Tony is very, very visible, right? And so, they would see Tony. First of all, if they're of a certain age or they haven't paid any attention before, they think they're seeing something new, right? And it's very well done, but it's not new, right? But now, only the serious student will start to move from that to even knowing, even being told, oh, part of this is NLP, and NLP comes from Bandler and Grindler. Well, only a serious student is now going to go find Bandler and Grindler stuff. And then when they find it's academic and dull and has charts that look like it's from MIT, they're going to bail out, they're not going to dig into it. And only a real serious student is going to say, well, where the hell did Bandler and Grindler get it? And now I'm going to go look for that. Same thing with copywriting, same thing with selling, same thing, you know, with anything. There's a real issue of sort of determined resourcefulness of, and you can see how people react to sort of every life event. And a small number of people go to work on it, right? And then a big hunk of people look for somebody to solve it for them, preferably in a simple and easy way, like you got a pill. And the majority of people do nothing about it, they just suffer unhappily. So real random chance, but then a great degree of efficacy in what is there.
